summary: Large decompilation and typing pass for src/melee/lb/lb_00F9, covering library dynamics, JObj traversal helpers, GX image/camera-blur rendering, and color overlay command handling. The PR replaced several stubs or weak prototypes with C implementations and refined shared DynamicsData layout; decomp.dev reported +3012 matched bytes, 7 new matches, and 11 unmatched-item improvements. Human review evidence is weak in this slice: the PR body only says "Tracking PR as usual" and there are no human review comments.

key_files.role: Main decompilation work: dynamics setup/update functions, JObj traversal, image/GX blur helpers, color overlay update, and GX drawing helpers.
Retyped DynamicsData and PolymorphicDesc layouts, removed ftDynamics-specific union member, typed global pool arrays, and refined camera blur data fields.
Replaced UNK prototypes with typed signatures for lb_800122F0 and lb_800138EC.
Added static globals for colors and a dynamics pause/flag byte: lb_804D3768, lb_804D376C, lb_804D63B8.
Changed lbVector_CreateEulerMatrix parameter type from Vec3* to Quaternion*.
Header signature synchronized with the Quaternion-based lbVector_CreateEulerMatrix implementation.
Updated fighter dynamic-bone references from desc.ft_unk.jobj to desc.lb_unk0.jobj.
Updated Castle stage dynamic-data reference from desc.ft_unk.jobj to desc.lb_unk0.jobj.

what_changed: Implemented or substantially decompiled many lb_00F9 functions, including lb_8000FA94, lb_8000FD48, lb_8001044C, lb_80011C18, lb_80011E24, lb_8001204C, lb_800122F0, lb_80012994, fn_80013614, lb_800138EC, lb_80014258, and lb_80014770.
decomp.dev reported 7 new full matches in main/melee/lb/lb_00F9: lb_80014770, lb_80014258, lb_800138EC, lb_8001204C, lb_80011710, lb_8001271C, and lb_800100B0.

decomp.dev also reported large partial improvements for unmatched items such as lb_8001044C from 0.00% to 81.26%, lb_80012994 from 0.00% to 99.57%, lb_800122F0 from 0.00% to 91.72%, and lb_8000FD48 from 0.00% to 93.66%.

DynamicsData was expanded from 0x94 to 0x98 with unk_94, and its union member for dynamic bones was generalized from ft_unk to lb_unk0. Existing fighter and Castle references were updated to the new name.

lb_00F9_UnkDesc0 was given concrete HSD_JObj, Quaternion, Vec3, and f32/s32 fields for saved rotation, translation, scale, world position, angular state, segment length, limits, and gravity/force parameters.
lb_804D63A0_t and lb_804D63A8_t changed from raw padding to typed entries arrays: DynamicsData entries[0x140] and lb_80011A50_t entries[8], preserving their static asserted sizes.
lb_8000FA94 now initializes linked free lists for the DynamicsData and lb_80011A50_t pools and clears globals such as cur_data, lb_804D63AC, lb_804D63B0, and lb_804D63B4.
lb_8000FD48 now builds a linked DynamicsData chain from a JObj child chain, saves initial JObj transforms, computes world positions, segment lengths, and dominant axes.

lb_8001044C became a large C dynamics solver using matrices, vector normalization/cross products, quaternions, stiffness/gravity/force blending, collider avoidance, floor checks, and JObj rotation updates.
GX/camera-blur work added lb_800122F0 TEV setup, lb_80012994 multi-offset blur drawing, fn_80013614 render callback logic, and lb_800138EC setup of a camera GObj and user data.
Color overlay command stepping was decompiled in lb_80014258 using Command_Execute and the lb_803BA248 handler table, then blending color/light channels and lifetime counters.
lbVector_CreateEulerMatrix was retyped to accept Quaternion* instead of Vec3*, matching the JObj rotation and saved rotation fields now used by lb_00F9 dynamics.
smart_moves: Renaming the shared union member from ft_unk to lb_unk0 avoided a fighter-specific name for data that is also used by lb_00F9 and grcastle, making the type model less misleading for future library and stage work.

Typing the global pool storage as arrays of real structs, while preserving STATIC_ASSERT sizes, made allocator/free-list code in lb_8000FA94 and lb_8000FD48 understandable without sacrificing layout guarantees.
Changing lbVector_CreateEulerMatrix to Quaternion* captured the actual semantics of HSD_JObj rotation fields even though the helper name says Euler, reducing casts and improving downstream typing.

The JObj index lookup helpers reuse the previous traversal index when requested indices are increasing and reset to root when indices decrease; this matches the visible control-flow intent and avoids repeatedly traversing from root unnecessarily.
Landing a high-quality C skeleton for the huge lb_8001044C dynamics solver exposed many field meanings and call dependencies even though the bot still classified it as an unmatched improvement.
The GX blur code was written with explicit repeated draw calls and alpha constants rather than an abstract loop, which is often necessary for matching original codegen in small rendering helpers.
decomp_lessons: When a union member is shared across subsystems, prefer a neutral library-scoped name such as lb_unk0 over a guessed subsystem name like ft_unk.
For data-pool globals, replacing padding with typed entries arrays is safe when accompanied by exact STATIC_ASSERT size checks and can unlock allocator/deallocator matches.
Do not rely only on structural compatibility between Vec3 and Quaternion; use the semantic type that matches call sites and HSD fields.
Large physics/dynamics functions can teach struct layouts before they fully match; record partial match percentages so future work knows what is still unresolved.
GX helper matches are sensitive to exact state setup order, TEV stage choices, stack padding, and unrolled drawing patterns.
Cross-file cleanup is required after union/layout changes: fighter and stage users had to move from desc.ft_unk.jobj to desc.lb_unk0.jobj in the same PR.
naming_conventions: Address-based function names were retained for unknown functions: lb_8000FD48, lb_8001044C, fn_80013614, lb_80014770.
Static globals keep address-style names and comments in static headers, e.g. lb_804D3768, lb_804D376C, lb_804D63B8.
Unknown struct fields remain offset-oriented unk_XX names, but typed fields such as jobj, rotate, translate, and scale were introduced when evidence was clear.
PolymorphicDesc variants use lb_unk0 and lb_unk1 after removing the misleading ftDynamics_UnkDesc/ft_unk member.
Public prototypes in lb_00F9.h keep address comments like /* 0122F0 */ while replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS when signatures become known.
Helper/local types use library-scoped names such as struct lb_Collider and struct lb_800138D8_t.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK in multiple functions, including PAD_STACK(0x20), PAD_STACK(88), PAD_STACK(16), and PAD_STACK(8), to satisfy stack/codegen constraints.
Applied #pragma inline_depth(2) around lb_80011C18 and restored #pragma inline_depth(8), indicating inline-depth control was part of the matching strategy.
Adjusted signedness and return value in inlineA1: s32 max, explicit casts against cur->x1, and returning ret instead of cur.
Preserved exact casts and branch shapes such as (u8) lb_804D63B8, (s32) desc->count < (s32) max_count, and (u32) arg1 == 2U.
Used explicit PSMTXTrans, PSMTXConcat, PSMTXScale, PSMTXQuat, EulerToQuat, PSVECCrossProduct, and lbVector_RotateAboutUnitAxis calls to mirror the original dynamics math pipeline.
Kept unrolled GX draw calls and TEV/alpha state changes in lb_80012994 rather than abstracting the repeated blur samples into a loop.
Introduced static vector/color constants such as lb_803B7280, lb_803B72A8, lb_803B72B4, lb_804D3768, and lb_804D376C to account for rodata/sdata/sbss changes.
review_feedback: No human review comments or GitHub reviews are present in the slice.
PR body was minimal: "Tracking PR as usual".
Automated decomp.dev report for GALE01 showed matched code at 62.41%, +0.08%, +3012 bytes.
Automated report listed 7 new matches and 11 improvements in unmatched items, validating that the large lb_00F9 pass improved matching even where some functions remained partial.
